name: Platinum
description_short: Platinum Educational Group provides online testing, scheduling, competency tracking, and compliance reporting software for EMS, nursing, and allied health educational institutions and programs.
description_long:
Platinum Educational Group was founded in 2002 by Doug Smith and Tom Gottschalk and is headquartered in Grand Rapids, Michigan. The company specializes in serving the medical education sector, particularly institutions and programs in Emergency Medical Services (EMS), Nursing, and Allied Health fields. Over two decades, Platinum has built a suite of software solutions designed to streamline operations and support student success in healthcare education.

Platinum's core offerings include EMSTesting (their flagship product for EMS professionals since 2002), Platinum Tests (launched 2016 for allied health institutions), Platinum Planner (scheduling software across EMS, nursing, and allied health), the Platinum Planner Mobile App, and AccredAssist® (accreditation management software launched 2019). The company also operates an educational workshops program, publishes monthly newsletters, produces video content showcasing customer use cases, and administers scholarship programs for students in EMS, nursing, and allied health programs.

Platinum distinguishes itself through founder-led commitment to medical education quality, long operational history, and deep specialization in a narrow vertical. The company offers CAPCE-accredited workshops, multiple scholarship awards (including the 2024 Smith Award, Gottschalk Award, and 2026 Susan Jensen Award), and maintains direct relationships with educational institutions rather than serving consumers. Their products focus on institutional compliance, competency tracking, and accreditation support rather than consumer financial services.

This is a B2B educational software company, not a consumer finance provider. The miscategorization as "payday-alternatives" appears to be a data error. Platinum serves college program directors and educational institutions managing healthcare professional training programs, making it fundamentally misaligned with consumer financial services categories.
services: EMSTesting online testing platform for EMS professionals
Platinum Tests assessment software for allied health institutions
Platinum Planner scheduling software for EMS, nursing, and allied health
Platinum Planner Mobile App for mobile scheduling access
AccredAssist® accreditation reporting and document management
Online competency tracking and compliance reporting
CAPCE-accredited virtual workshops and professional development
Platinum Scholarship Program for EMS, nursing, and allied health students
Monthly educational newsletters and product updates
pros: Specialized expertise in medical education spanning EMS, nursing, and allied health sectors
Established 22-year track record since 2002 with founder-led commitment to quality
Comprehensive suite including testing, scheduling, competency tracking, and accreditation support
CAPCE-accredited educational workshops for professional development
Active scholarship program supporting next-generation healthcare professionals
Software designed specifically for institutional compliance and accreditation reporting
Maintains active customer engagement through video series, newsletters, and community building
cons: Not a consumer finance product — serves educational institutions, not individual borrowers
No transparent pricing information available on public website
Limited information about integration capabilities with other institutional systems
Appears to be a niche vertical player without broader financial services offerings
best_for: College program directors managing EMS, nursing, or allied health accreditation
Educational institutions needing competency tracking and compliance reporting software
Healthcare training programs seeking CAPCE-accredited professional development
diagnosis: Platinum Educational Group is a B2B educational software provider, not a consumer finance company. The categorization as a payday alternative is factually incorrect — this company serves educational institutions managing healthcare professional training, not consumers seeking loans or financial products. Any consumer considering this listing should recognize it is not relevant to personal financial services needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
